item.title: Aftersun, 2022 - ★★★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description: I miss.., too many people

item.title: Lee Cronin's The Mummy, 2026 - ★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description: spoopy and goopy and boopy and floopy

item.title: The Naked Gun, 2025 - ★★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
My dad asked me for "dinner and a movie" so I took him out for a steak dinner and then we watched this action/comedy. This was not a bad pick!

Watched with dad

item.title: Disclosure Day, 2026 - ★★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
a fun time was had! mostly by me. other folks checked their phones every 30 minutes.

nothing really novel here but the third act feels really good. Spielberg had fun with it, too. maybe he was milking it a bit, but I enjoyed and believed Emily Blunt and Josh O'Connor too much to not feel it!

maybe dial it back about 50% on the lens flares, though, bud

Watched with Corey, Brandon, and Ryan

item.title: Master of the Universe, 2026 - ★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
good fun and hot actors

I will be seated for a sequel

item.title: The Drama, 2026 - ★★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
This sticks a solid 4 for me but I do wonder if the theme of performative nature could be explored more

Watched with Jennie

item.title: Steel Magnolias, 1989 - ★★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
“Drum, eat shit and die.”

I loved so many moments in this.
Fantastic performances all around.
I needed about 5 minutes taken off the end.

Sally Field was the throughline for me — I understood the emotional weight of the plot based on her character.

I laughed and I cried. This is why I love the movies.

item.title: Obsession, 2025 - ★★★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
Inde Navarette’s beautiful face will haunt my nightmares tonight 

Watched with Ryan and Andy

item.title: A Murder at the End of the World, 2023 - ★★½
item.ns0:filmTitle: A Murder at the End of the World
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description: I liked a good bit of this but struggled to really believe it.

item.title: Dune: Part Two, 2024 - ★★★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description: Dimalā-shūdhī, el isnīhd.Rū el isnīhd, el isnīhd — rūdhī el isnīBellā ha'rudhī al dimbīsh.Rū al dimbīsh, al dimbī — rūdhī al dimbīshRu kashāh, chifthit, el isnīhd.Rū el isniidh, el isniidh — rūdhī el isnī

item.title: Power Ballad, 2026 - ★★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
Fantastic music comedy with the pals 

Watched with Eddie, Mack, Micah, and Jeana

item.title: The Whale, 2022 - ★★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
Okay, I think I can finally admit this film is good

It's free on YouTube

item.title: The Immortal, 2019 - ★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description: Watched on Tuesday June 2, 2026.

item.title: The History of Sound, 2025 - ★★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
i love folk music. i love paul mescal & josh o'connor. i love sam amidon.

i hope that my 4-star rating indicates that this is an excellent film worth taking the time to watch, regardless your personal interest in folk music, this film shows a queer love story that is complicated, historic, and true.

happy pride month, from me to you 🏳️‍🌈

item.title: Is God Is, 2026 - ★★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
I love a good revenge story

Watched with Bear

item.title: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, 2026 - ★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description: we've had two princesses, yes. but what about third princess?

item.title: Fallen Angels, 1995 - ★★★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
"They say women are made of water. So are some men. Most people fall in love for the first time as teenagers. I guess I'm a late bloomer. Maybe I am too picky. On May 30, 1995, I finally fell in love for the first time. It was raining that night."

watched so i could do the thing where you log a movie just because it calls out a specific date in an iconic line

"the night's full of weirdos"

masturbation is such a healthy way to process your feelings — I am pro-masturbation scenes in movies

item.title: Flower - ★★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description: Watched on Saturday May 30, 2026.

item.title: Backrooms, 2026 - ★★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
I won't say I'm entirely disappointed but it does feel like an extended Kane Pixels YouTube upload just with the most minimalist plot conceived for it. I would have loved if the ending was actually the opening of the final act -- and then really went NUTS.

But instead it only felt liminal. 

A one-note atmosphere is fine for a short; I needed to hear at least three notes to strike a chord. 

Watched with Ryan.

item.title: I Love Boosters, 2026 - ★★★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
outrageous color and ridiculously fun

a little less substantial than Sorry to Bother You but it absolutely commits to the style, and I loved the style

item.title: Wake Up Dead Man, 2025 - ★★★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
So I've done the past hour doing exactly that, and now I'll put down this pen and hand it to you, and I guess wait, while you read my story of the murder of Monsignor Jefferson Wicks

This quote coming from Father Jud just after the hour turns on the runtime is 👌🏻 chef's kiss

item.title: In the Grey, 2026 - ★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
Henry Cavill looks good so that’s one reason to watch this

The writing is pretty shitty but the action is fun

Watch this movie if you want to forget it

item.title: The Last Waltz, 1978 - ★★★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
This movie told me to play it loud and so I did

Title #2 in the MUBI Collection "Martin Scorsese: The Early Years"

item.title: Boxcar Bertha, 1972 - ★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
terribly sad ending

Title #1 in the MUBI Collection "Martin Scorsese: The Early Years"

item.title: The Truman Show, 1998 - ★★★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description: Watched on Thursday May 21, 2026.

item.title: Obsession, 2025 - ★★★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
Curry Barker has such a good control of the tone during this, at times I was both horrified and amused. I want to rewatch and really pay attention to the editing because lots of little things kept adding on to the tension.

And what a love story! It's like Romeo & Juliet 💀

Also, to the guy in the Pop Tarts pajama bottoms next to me who said, "I'm so confused" at the end credits: you didn't watch the movie! You were on your phone the whole time — of course you're confused, you're trying to understand something you completely ignored.

Watched with Azia, Micah, Eddie, and Mackenzie

item.title: The Fabelmans, 2022 - ★★★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description: i miss this

item.title: All the President's Men, 1976 - ★★★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description: actually makes me rethink The Devil Wears Prada films and their perspective on journalistic standards

item.title: Eddington, 2025 - ★★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
first time rewatching since my theater experience.

there were some moments that had me laughing out loud.

great filmmaking.

item.title: Lurker, 2025 - ★★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description: oooh that ending scratched such a specific itch

item.title: Treadmill - ★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description: Watched on Friday May 15, 2026.

item.title: Atropia, 2025 - ★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description: what is this movie

item.title: Wuthering Heights, 2026 - ★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description: it was long and i got so bored i fell asleep for five minutes or so and did not care

item.title: Would You Like to Try Again?, 2019 - ★★★
item.ns0:filmTitle: Would You Like to Try Again?
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description: Watched on Wednesday May 13, 2026.

item.title: The Sheep Detectives, 2026 - ★★★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
Really wonderful and charming. I went with Mary and then Ben and Elizabeth crashed our friend date and joined us. 

The sheep are the stars, and the animation felt really good. I loved all the jokes referencing children’s nursery rhymes, and generally I found the humor to work for me.

I wish I had a little more Hugh Jackman but it’s still impressive how effective he was at getting me acquainted and in love with the sheep — though probably the hardest thing for me to believe is that a man with those biceps is a vegetarian. 

Great film.

item.title: Short Circuit, 1986 - ★★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
"Jerk of the world"

I haven't seen this since I was a kid and I remember loving it — I think I understand why now. Number 5 learns about death and feels anxious about it. Infinitely relatable.

But it's what he learns after that feeling that makes his story so compelling.

item.title: A Son's Wedding, 2024 - ★★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
dripping in ennui but ultimately sweet 

happy Mother’s Day

item.title: Hot Rod, 2007 - ★★★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
you guys, the bathroom here is nuts

dave is me

item.title: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple, 2026 - ★★★★★

"do we help them?"

"of course we do."

absolutely rips

item.title: The Devil Wears Prada 2, 2026 - ★★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
well-written and lovingly crafted.

i won tickets to see this from a giveaway hosted by Mississippi Film Society. Abigail joined.

item.title: Marty Supreme, 2025 - ★★★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description: I got blue balled by a brewery buddy who bailed an hour into this film. i'm just now finishing it, 8 days later.

item.title: Memory Project, 2026 - ★★★★
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
The Mligram Experiment was such a captivating story to me. I was studying for my Computer Ethics course at the time, so it felt like a complementary read, thematically.

This short film caused me to re-live much of the emotions I felt learning about how the experiments worked. I found it effective.

Wathced via Omeleto on YouTube

item.title: Interstellar, 2014 - ★★★★½
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
the moment Cooper stops to look at the ships while on Cooper Station — it is already necessary for him to leave.

the daughter who loved him enough to know — do not go gentle into that good night — sends her father away to another planet.

item.title: Movies Recommended To Me

    Steven Nalley

  • Fighting with My Family

  • Bloody Hell

    Charlie (FP)

  • Stay Out of the Attic

    Charlie (FP)

item.title: JXNFC: Michael's Top 20 (2025 REDUX)
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
Five years ago, I set down my Top 20 Favorites and then started a podcast where I collected other people’s Top 20s and had them talk about it on the internet. This is an updated list, not invalidating the previous Top 20, just memorializing my current Top 20.

If you’re reading this, I’d love to know your Top 20 favorites.

  1. The Truman Show
  2. Spirited Away
  3. Aftersun
  4. First Reformed
  5. Moonlight
  6. Dune: Part Two
  7. Blade Runner 2049
  8. Sunshine
  9. Midsommar
  10. Drive

...plus 10 more. View the full list on Letterboxd.

item.title: JXNFC: Michael Lamb's Top 20 (2020)
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
Michael loves rewatching movies. Those movies become his favorites. Those favorites make up this list.

  1. The Truman Show
  2. Spirited Away
  3. Princess Mononoke
  4. First Reformed
  5. Moonlight
  6. Hereditary
  7. Ex Machina
  8. Blade Runner 2049
  9. Midsommar
  10. Manchester by the Sea

...plus 10 more. View the full list on Letterboxd.

item.title: Bear's November Movie Challenge
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
Bear challenged me to list movies with a "November" vibe. Bear gave a subjective trifecta but I've forgotten it (please add it in a comment, thank you!)

My interpretation of these vibes led me to review my watchlist as well as my diary and I sought these qualities:
- Visual: muted/desaturated, bare trees, overcast skies, early darkness
- Emotional: contemplative melancholy, not depression exactly but... reckoning
- Narrative: moral ambiguity, isolation, characters alone with consequences
- Pacing: patient, meditative, space to think

Did I succeed?

  • Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
  • Manchester by the Sea
  • The Wind Will Carry Us
  • Fargo
  • Mulholland Drive
  • In Bruges
  • The Witch
  • A Serious Man
  • First Reformed
  • A Ghost Story

...plus 4 more. View the full list on Letterboxd.

item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:

  • Meshes of the Afternoon
  • Arsenic and Old Lace

  • The Spiral Staircase
  • Black Narcissus
  • Rope

item.title: JXNFC: Jake Sullivan’s Top 20
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
• Cool Hand Luke

    A crash course in humanity. 

    "No man can eat 50 eggs."

  • The Godfather

    A crash course in film. Perfect in every aspect.

  • Blood Simple

    #3 is a revolving door of Coen films. This spot belongs to whichever I'm feeling the most on a given day. Their debut is underappreciated and seems like an appropriate placeholder.

  • Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid

    Every top 20 should have a western. And the best westerns are about bucking authority and beating the odds.

  • The Truman Show

    My life is definitely not filled by paid actors. But my brain thinks it might be.

  • Big Night

    The movie that taught me to appreciate food. And business. And Louie Prima.

    "Bite your teeth into the ass of life."

  • White Men Can't Jump

    Iconic 90s.

  • Thicker Than Water

    When I am wistful, surf docs settle my soul. This one is my go to.

  • Good Will Hunting

    Robin Williams at his very best and most vulnerable. RIP.

  • Attack the Block

    Pure fun. Easily my favorite monster movie.

...plus 10 more. View the full list on Letterboxd.

item.title: JXNFC: Mark Goins’ Top 20
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
These are the films that have influenced me throughout my life, from the ages of 0 to today. I consider them to be my most rewatchable films of all time. I’m sure this list will change, but this is it right now.And yes, LOTR is one movie. No question.
  • The Mask of Zorro
  • The Prestige
  • Spider-Man
  • Spider-Man 2
  • The Social Network
  • The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
  • The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers
  • The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
  • The Godfather
  • The Godfather Part II

...plus 12 more. View the full list on Letterboxd.

item.title: JXNFC: Ben Wilson's Top 20
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
Ben's list is in no particular order and includes five films deserving honorable mention. Classics, pop culture phenomenons, indie darlings, thought-provoking cinematic features, buddy comedy. This list has it all.

  • Beauty and the Beast
  • Prisoners
  • The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
  • The Empire Strikes Back
  • It Happened One Night
  • It's a Wonderful Life
  • Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
  • The Shawshank Redemption
  • Lost in Translation
  • Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl

...plus 15 more. View the full list on Letterboxd.

item.title: JXNFC: Jonathan Miles’ Top 20 Films
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
Classics, action, comedy, fantasy. This list has a little of everything.

  1. The Big Lebowski

    The greatest film ever made, bar none. Shut the fuck up, Donny

  2. The Nice Guys

    Just two guys, in way over their heads.

  3. There Will Be Blood

    One of the greatest Method acting performances of all time, although my contention will ever be that Daniel Day-Lewis deserved his second Oscar before this for Gangs of New York.

  4. Hell or High Water

    The best Texas noir film since No Country For Old Men.

  5. Pride & Prejudice

    A stirringly scored, beautifully shot adaptation of the Austen novel.

  6. The Searchers

    A visually brilliant, brutally spectacular film, and the performance that should have won John Wayne his Academy Award, for bitter, racist ex-Confederate Ethan Edwards.

  7. Zodiac

    David Fincher’s masterpiece. A stacked cast and a case that was never solved.

  8. When Harry Met Sally...

    The ultimate rom-com

  9. Talladega Nights: The Ballad of Ricky Bobby

    If you eat the DVD, it tastes like America

  10. Silver Linings Playbook

    Go Birds

...plus 10 more. View the full list on Letterboxd.

item.title: JXNFC: Quentin Jones' Top 20
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
Quentin Jones, friend of the film club, listed his top 20 on the most recent episode of Jackson Film Club The Podcast.

Quentin found himself in Jackson after working on the film crew for the episode of We Are Here created in Jackson in 2022. His first and foremost reason for his residence in the city is the love of his life, Lydia. His second reason is his mission for cannabis awareness and advancement in the state's new medical marijuana program. His third reason is the great dance floor at Shade.

This top 20 list features epic American classics and some of the most innovative and groundbreaking cinema in the world. Tune in to our conversation wherever you get your podcasts.

Though this is presented as a ranked list, the films in positions 22 - 24 are honorable mentions and despicable artists-now-canceled.

  1. Get Out
  2. The Big Lebowski
  3. In the Mood for Love
  4. Hairspray
  5. Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse
  6. Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse
  7. Blue Velvet
  8. Do the Right Thing
  9. Moonlight
  10. Barbie

...plus 14 more. View the full list on Letterboxd.

item.title: JXNFC: Sam Graef’s Top 20
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
There’s a lot of uncertainty here, and I feel the need to explain myself but I won’t. Top 2 are in order and then the rest are give and take.

- Sam

  • The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
  • The Social Network
  • The Place Beyond the Pines
  • No Country for Old Men
  • Zodiac
  • 12 Angry Men
  • Inception
  • Tenet
  • La La Land
  • The Prestige

...plus 10 more. View the full list on Letterboxd.

item.title: JXNFC - Animated Features
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
This list was the basis of a special podcast episode. Check the link for more

https://jxnfilm.club/#animated-features

  • The Land Before Time

    Entered by Michael

    This film is my waypoint for consciousness -- I came to an understanding of death emotionally because of this movie. Nothing has ever quite hit the same, to be honest.

  • Rock-A-Doodle

    Entered by Michael

    When my parents would go to their weekly bowling league, I was the kid who would change the TV to Rock-A-Doodle at the childcare center next door.

  • One Hundred and One Dalmatians

    Entered by Michael

  • Princess Mononoke

    Entered by Michael

    This was when I realized that animation is for grownups, and that grownup stories can be told through animation. I love how this changed my imagination.

  • Spirited Away

    Entered by Michael

    I found this not far from the age of Chihiro. It means a lot to me because I moved twice before the age of 10 and this film teaches about life in its own way, through the eyes of a young person who has been Spirited Away.

  • Samurai Champloo

    Entered by Michael

    This anime popped up on Adult Swim, but I never finished the series on the air. Nearly 20 years later, I've finally watched the entirety and absolutely loved the ending.

  • The Hobbit

    Entered by Morgan

  • Heavy Metal

    Entered by Morgan

  • Waking Life

    Entered by Morgan

  • The Secret of Kells

    Entered by Morgan

...plus 1 more. View the full list on Letterboxd.

item.title: 2024 Oxford Film Festival
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
I attended the 2024 Oxford Film Festival and got the opportunity to interview a number of the filmmakers for Jackson Film Club. You'll get the chance to see those soon!

Here are all the films I could find listed on Letterboxd. There were others I had the privilege of seeing but they're not yet available to journalize.

  • Infraction
  • Barely Breathing
  • The Old Young Crow
  • A Song For Imogene
  • Lee Baby
  • Amazing Grace
  • Endline
  • Rising Hope
  • Mississippi River Styx
  • Slower Animals

...plus 24 more. View the full list on Letterboxd.

Michael Tran is my manager at C Spire. We chat about movies. Here's a list he thought worth sharing with me.

  • Beyond the Black Rainbow
  • Boyhood
  • Brazil
  • Chungking Express
  • District 9
  • First Reformed
  • King of California
  • Manchester by the Sea
  • Martha Marcy May Marlene
  • Our Idiot Brother

...plus 10 more. View the full list on Letterboxd.

item.title: 2022 Goal: 100 New Directors
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
I started the year with a pretty interesting ratio of 1.28:1 for number of films by a director. On 14 October 2021 I reached a milestone of 500 directors from 646 logged films, spanning 41 countries, inspiring my 2022 goal of watching 100 movies introducing myself to that many more new directors. As of completion of this goal, my ratio is 1.33 movies per director with over 700 directors logged. I look forward to seeing where it's at when I hit 1500 movies logged

tl;dr: for every movie on this list, 2022 is the first time I’ve seen something from that director.

  1. The Hand of God
  2. The Lost Daughter
  3. Summer of Soul (...Or, When the Revolution Could Not Be Televised)
  4. The Mask of Zorro
  5. The Paradise Next Door
  6. Lunch on the Grass
  7. Doctor Strange
  8. The Good, the Bad and the Ugly
  9. A Bridge Too Far
  10. Encanto

...plus 89 more. View the full list on Letterboxd.

  9. The Wind Rises

item.title: JXNFC: Good Actors in Bad Movies
item.dc:creator: Michael Lamb
item.description:
This list is a collection of films discussed in the episode of JXN Film Club The Podcast Film Club Topic #3.

Contributors
Micah Smith
Sam Graef
Michael Lamb

  • Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker

    Actor: Adam Driver
    Submitted by Sam

  • Lincoln

    Actor: Daniel Day-Lewis
    Submitted by Sam

  • Gangs of New York

    Actor: Daniel Day-Lewis
    Submitted by Sam

  • Velvet Buzzsaw

    Actor: Jake Gyllenhaal
    Submitted by Sam

  • The Devil All the Time

    Actor: Robert Pattinson
    Submitted by Sam

  • Twilight

    Actor: Robert Pattinson & Kristen Stewart
    Hot Take Submitted by Michael

  • Malevolent

    Actor: Florence Pugh
    Esoteric Submission by Michael

  • Snowden

    Actor: Joseph Gordon-Levitt
    Modern Submission by Michael

  • Encounter

    Actor: Riz Ahmed
    New Release Submission by Michael

  • The Amazing Spider-Man

    Actor: Andrew Garfield & Emma Stone
    Submitted by Sam

...plus 11 more. View the full list on Letterboxd.
